# Regression test for two real incidents: an unrecognized `--flag`-shaped token
# (most commonly a bare --help/-h) used to silently land in a required
# positional slot instead of being rejected -- `breeze identity register --help`
# registered a real identity literally named "--help" (leaking its token to
# stdout), and `breeze lock acquire --help` silently acquired a real exclusive
# lock on the literal path "--help". Neither must ever happen again, for any
# subcommand, not just the ones originally reported.
env BREEZE_DIR=$WORK/state

# --help exits cleanly (0) and just prints usage — no mutation.
exec breeze identity register --help
stdout 'usage: breeze identity register'
exec breeze ps --json
stdout '\{\}'

exec breeze lock acquire --help
stdout 'usage: breeze lock acquire'
exec breeze lock list --json
stdout '"locks":\s*\[\]'

# A genuine typo'd flag must error clearly too, not silently register/acquire
# using the flag text as the intended value.
! exec breeze identity register alice --tokne foo
stderr 'unrecognized flag "--tokne"'
exec breeze ps --json
stdout '\{\}'

exec breeze stop
